data.content:
### RunsOn Guides

Source: https://runs-on.com/docs/index

Provides links to guides for installing, troubleshooting, upgrading, downgrading, and uninstalling RunsOn.

```markdown
[ Installation ](https://runs-on.com/guides/install/) RunsOn is the best way to run GitHub Actions in your own AWS infrastructure, and get 10x cheaper runners.
[ Troubleshooting ](https://runs-on.com/guides/troubleshoot/) Troubleshoot your RunsOn installation
[ Upgrade ](https://runs-on.com/guides/upgrade/) Learn how to upgrade RunsOn.
[ Downgrade ](https://runs-on.com/guides/downgrade/) Learn how to downgrade RunsOn.
[ Uninstall ](https://runs-on.com/guides/uninstall/) Learn how to uninstall RunsOn.
```

--------------------------------

### RunsOn Guides

Source: https://runs-on.com/docs/index

Guides for using RunsOn, covering troubleshooting, upgrading, downgrading, uninstalling, best practices, building custom AMIs, CLI usage, and language-specific guides.

```markdown
- [Troubleshooting](https://runs-on.com/guides/troubleshoot/)
- [Upgrade](https://runs-on.com/guides/upgrade/)
- [Downgrade](https://runs-on.com/guides/downgrade/)
- [Uninstall](https://runs-on.com/guides/uninstall/)
- [Best Practices](https://runs-on.com/guides/best-practices/)
- [Building a custom AMI with Packer](https://runs-on.com/guides/building-custom-ami-with-packer/)
- [CLI](https://runs-on.com/guides/cli/)
- [Rust](https://runs-on.com/guides/languages/rust/)
```

--------------------------------

### RunsOn Runner Setup

Source: https://runs-on.com/docs/index

Instructions for setting up self-hosted runners for GitHub Actions, including support for Linux, Windows, and GPU instances.

```markdown
[ Linux ](https://runs-on.com/runners/linux/) Learn how to set up and use Linux self-hosted runners for your GitHub Actions workflows. Supports native x64 and arm64 architectures.
[ Windows ](https://runs-on.com/runners/windows/) Learn how to set up and use Windows self-hosted runners for your GitHub Actions workflows.
[ GPU ](https://runs-on.com/runners/gpu/) 10x cheaper GPU runners with NVIDIA or AMD GPUs for GitHub Actions.
```

--------------------------------

### Mastering GitHub Actions with RunsOn

Source: https://runs-on.com/docs/index

A guide to mastering GitHub Actions when using RunsOn, covering introduction, triggers, pull request nuances, jobs and steps, conditions, environment variables, matrix strategy, caching, environments, cross-workflow triggers, debugging, larger runners, and self-hosted runners.

```markdown
- [Introduction](https://runs-on.com/github-actions/introduction/)
- [Triggers](https://runs-on.com/github-actions/triggers/)
- [Pull Request vs Pull Request Target](https://runs-on.com/github-actions/pull-request-vs-pull-request-target/)
- [Jobs and Steps](https://runs-on.com/github-actions/jobs-and-steps/)
- [Conditions](https://runs-on.com/github-actions/conditions/)
- [Environment Variables](https://runs-on.com/github-actions/environment-variables/)
- [The Matrix Strategy](https://runs-on.com/github-actions/the-matrix-strategy/)
- [Caching Dependencies](https://runs-on.com/github-actions/caching-dependencies/)
- [Environments](https://runs-on.com/github-actions/environments/)
- [Triggering a Workflow from Another Workflow](https://runs-on.com/github-actions/triggering-a-workflow-from-another-workflow/)
- [Debugging](https://runs-on.com/github-actions/debug/)
- [Larger Runners](https://runs-on.com/github-actions/larger-runners/)
- [Self-hosted Runners](https://runs-on.com/github-actions/self-hosted-runners/)
```

Details on configuring RunsOn, including job labels, repository settings, stack configuration, environments, custom images, resource tags, and spot instances.

```markdown

- [Repository configuration](https://runs-on.com/configuration/repo-config/)
```

--------------------------------

### RunsOn Configuration Options

Source: https://runs-on.com/docs/index

Details on configuring RunsOn, including job labels, repository settings, stack configurations, environments, custom images, GitHub App permissions, and resource tags.

```markdown
[ Job labels ](https://runs-on.com/configuration/job-labels/) Learn how to configure your GitHub Actions runners with specific requirements in terms of CPU, GPU, RAM, disk size, and more.
[ Repository configuration ](https://runs-on.com/configuration/repo-config/) Learn all about .github/runs-on.yml configuration file
[ Stack configuration ](https://runs-on.com/configuration/stack-config/) Learn how to configure your RunsOn stack.
[ Environments ](https://runs-on.com/configuration/environments/) Learn how to use RunsOn environments to isolate different workflows in different environments, with different IAM permissions or stack configurations, etc.
[ Custom images ](https://runs-on.com/configuration/custom-images/) RunsOn provides compatible images with official GitHub Actions runners, but you can also bring your own images if you want!
[ GitHub App Permissions ](https://runs-on.com/configuration/updating-github-app-permissions/) Learn how to manually update permissions for your GitHub App when a RunsOn upgrade requires new permissions.
[ Resource tags ](https://runs-on.com/configuration/resource-tags/) Learn how to tag RunsOn resources with custom tags
```

--------------------------------

### RunsOn Caching Strategies

Source: https://runs-on.com/docs/index

Explains various caching strategies in RunsOn, including Magic Cache, ephemeral registry, S3 cache backend, and snapshots for optimizing build performance.

```markdown
[ Overview ](https://runs-on.com/caching/) Overview of the different caching strategies available in RunsOn, and how to use them.
[ Magic Cache ✨ ](https://runs-on.com/caching/magic-cache/) How to use RunsOn Magic Cache to speed up your builds and get unlimited cache on GitHub Actions runners, by swapping the cache backend to S3.
[ Ephemeral registry ](https://runs-on.com/caching/ephemeral-registry/) How to use the ephemeral registry with RunsOn, for incredible build speeds
[ runs-on/cache@v4 ](https://runs-on.com/caching/s3-cache-for-github-actions/) Use an S3 bucket as a cache backend for your actions, to enjoy faster download and upload speeds + unlimited cache sizes
[ Snapshots ](https://runs-on.com/caching/snapshots/) Save and restore entire folders with block-level snapshots. Helps to speed-up docker builds in your GitHub Actions workflows.
```

--------------------------------
